reason: Response A demonstrates the tighter thematic control and restraint I associate with Opus: a single arresting image (a river running backward) that resolves into a quiet moral statement about debts falling on "the poor and the patient," with vivid, economical specificity (forty years, two wives, silver confused fish). Response B is competent but leans on more conventional, slightly overworked moves (em-dash piling, "crystallized into," the italicized note, a two-paragraph hook with a deferred reveal) that read more like the formulaic atmosphere-building typical of a smaller model. The disciplined imagery and earned final beat in A point to it being mine.
